notes:
Everything works perfectly out of the box, including gamepad vibrations (XBOX 360 controller).
Performance is much better than "native" OpenGL version, especially in CPU bound parts of the game. Plus the Windows version has access to CMAA antialiasing which is more efficient than MSAA.
Only oddity, the builtin benchmarks runs capped at 12fps until you bring the pause menu. It then proceeds to work flawlessly. Weird :)
title: DiRT Rally

notes:
Wine D3d11 is required to start the game, but stages fail to load entirely, requiring a killsig to terminate the game process. Given that one would be tempted to run the game through Proton because the performance delta between the native Windows version and the Feral port is abysmal, this is disappointing.

Other EGO titles allegedly exhibit similar behaviour under Proton.
title: DiRT Rally

notes:
Just FYI I run it through proton instead of native because I'd need to start from the beginning because windows save files aren't compatible with native Linux version.
Almost everything works as it should except online won't work and some texts are crispy but still readable like the speed in 3rd person. Runs well with ds4 controller both USB and Bluetooth through ds4drv. The game runs ~120fps or more almost all the time with medium-ish settings in 1440p, the only thing that will make it run 144 fps all the time is disabling shaders.
title: DiRT Rally

notes:
Obviously there's a native version of this game but it doesn't allow the loading of saves produced by the Windows version.
Playing the game via Proton works after setting "PROTON_USE_WINED3D=1" in the launch options. 
However when starting a track (or the benchmark) the game locks up during the loading screen. The audio keeps playing but the loading screen stalls and doesn't continue. A force close is required to exit the game after this.
title: DiRT Rally

notes: I have no issue's with this game running with proton. I posted a steamcommunity video at https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=gWU3IpgcHVY
title: DiRT Rally

notes: Although the game has a linux version, I decided to play it via Proton (Right click on the game > Properties > And tick "Force to use a specific Steam Play..." > Select proton version - I used 4.11.4 at the time of writing this -). The game works like a charm, on Ultra setting and consistent FPS (1080p). I also played it with a Xbox USB controller, detected automatically and it even had vibration feedback. Super fun and very hard game to play!
title: DiRT Rally

notes: I was getting lower FPS than expected using the native version (around 55 average at max settings using the benchmark tool) so I decided to try the proton version. To my surprise, my FPS about doubled when running with proton 4.11-5 (this version was just released today and is not yet an option on protondb). I averaged 116 fps with max settings using the benchmark utility. Everything worked with 0 configuration, in this case it was a lot better than the native version.
title: DiRT Rally

notes:
the only thing that looks like not working is the online mode but performance is better than native and more stable;

title: DiRT Rally
